text: When working with APIs, you send HTTP requests to retrieve or send data. Most modern APIs use JSON format to exchange information, which is easy to read and parse in many programming languages. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to identify who is making the request. You should also check the rate limits, which control how many requests you can make in a certain time. If you send too many requests, you may receive an error. Finally, always handle errors properly in your code. This helps your program respond correctly when something goes wrong, such as a failed connection or an invalid response.
